name: Functional Equivalence and Geometric Diversity in Neural Network Approximations: An Empirical Characterization

description: arXiv:2607.18930v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The Universal Approximation Theorem states that a neural network with a single hidden layer is sufficient to approximate any continuous univariate function on a compact domain to arbitrary error. However, the uniqueness of such neural network representations is not guaranteed, raising questions about practical identifiability. In this work, we a...
author: Anuragine S A, Prem Jagadeesan
